item.title: Svatantra Microfin Said to Prepare for $250 Million India IPO
item.description: Advent International LP-backed Svatantra Microfin Pvt. has met with bankers and lawyers as the first step in the process for an initial public offering that could raise as much as $250 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Zhipu Hikes Prices Again as China AI Monetization Wave Quickens
item.description: Zhipu raised the cost of access to its most advanced AI model by at least 8%, joining other leading Chinese artificial intelligence players in trying to profit off years of research and computing investments.

item.title: Traders Line Up Wagers on Hungary’s Markets as Election Nears
item.description: With just days to go before Hungarians head to the polls, investors and strategists say there’s more room for markets to rally if polls are correct in showing that Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year-old experiment in “illiberal democracy” is coming to an end.

item.title: A Civilization Doesn’t Die, and Markets Go Up
item.description: Crude has fallen more only during Covid and the outset of Desert Storm.

item.title: Vietnam Names New Central Bank Head as War Threatens Growth Goal
item.description: Vietnam named the ex-head of the nation’s biggest agricultural lender to run the central bank, which is tasked with turbocharging growth despite tariffs, inflationary pressures and the war in the Middle East.

item.title: India Keeps Key Rate Steady as Weak Rupee Takes Center Stage
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India held interest rates in its first policy decision since the Middle East crisis erupted, as it grapples with a sharply weaker rupee while trying to support economic growth.

item.title: AI K-Pop Startup Galaxy Aims For IPO in Seoul, New York
item.description: Humanoid robots dressed in black, luxury hip-hop outfits snap into formation, moving in perfect sync to K-pop songs. It’s a simulation inside an office styled as a crash-landed spacecraft in the heart of Seoul’s financial district, complete with towering astronauts and floating robotic jellyfish.

item.title: Bangladesh Bank Seeks to Calm FX Fears as Taka Hits Record Low
item.description: Bangladesh’s central bank sought to reassure investors that the nation’s foreign-exchange market remains stable and the taka faces no immediate risk of depreciation, even as the currency slid to a fresh low against the dollar.

item.title: Japan’s Chiyoda Considers Resuming Work on Qatar LNG Project
item.description: Japan’s Chiyoda Corp. says it is considering resuming construction work on a giant liquefied natural gas export plant in Qatar, in a sign of cautious optimism hours after the US and Iran agreed a ceasefire.

item.title: Chinese Battery Maker Announces 11B Yuan Capacity Expansion Plan
item.description: EVE Energy Co., one of China’s top lithium-ion battery makers, said it would build two new manufacturing plants with a combined annual capacity of 110 gigawatt-hours at a total cost of 11 billion yuan ($1.6 billion).

item.title: Indonesia’s FX Reserves Hit Two-Year Low on Rupiah Defense
item.description: Indonesia’s foreign-exchange reserves fell for a third straight month in March to the lowest level in nearly two years as the central bank stepped up market intervention to stabilize the falling rupiah.

item.title: Robot Maker Kuka Eyes US, Asia as Europe’s Factories Lag on AI
item.description: Many of Europe’s industrial companies are too slow to adopt artificial intelligence, putting faster-moving global rivals in a position to overtake them, according to German-Chinese robotics maker Kuka AG.

item.title: Emerging-Market Assets Rally as Iran Ceasefire Spurs Risk Demand
item.description: Emerging-market assets gained after a ceasefire deal between the US and Iran caused oil prices to plunge and revived risk appetite.

item.title: China Vanke Seeks to Delay Another Bond Payment to Avoid Default
item.description: Developer China Vanke Co. told some bondholders that it is seeking to extend a yuan bond due this month with an offer to repay 40% of the principal upfront, according to people familiar with the matter, as it again races to avoid default.

item.title: China’s Wasting Too Much Renewable Power as Curtailments Rise
item.description: China is wasting renewable power at an alarming rate, approaching limits that the government had relaxed only two years ago to accelerate solar and wind usage.

item.title: Colombia Deploys Subsidies After ‘Outrageous’ Central Bank Move
item.description: Colombia’s leftist government is rolling out a series of subsidies and cheap loans to limit the economic damage it says will result from the central bank’s “outrageous” interest rate policy.

item.title: Asian LNG Prices Set to Fall After US, Iran Agree to Ceasefire
item.description: Asian LNG prices are set to fall after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire that could temporarily reopen the Strait of Hormuz, bringing respite to global energy markets.

item.title: Indian Stocks May Extend 4-Day Winning Run as US, Iran Agree to a Ceasefire
item.description: Locally, focus is on RBI’s rate decision on Wednesday and TCS’ results Thursday.

item.title: Bain Data Center Unit Cuts Ties With Nvidia Buyer After US Probe
item.description: Bain Capital’s Bridge Data Centres has removed from its Malaysian computing hub a Southeast Asian company the US suspects of smuggling Nvidia Corp. chips.

item.title: New Zealand Holds Key Rate as Iran Fuel Shock Fans Inflation
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank held its key interest rate at an almost four-year low and restated it will look through the initial inflationary impact of surging fuel prices, in a decision released just hours after the US and Iran agreed to a tentative ceasefire.

item.title: China’s Yuan Climbs to Three-Year High on Iran Ceasefire Boost
item.description: China’s yuan hit a three-year high as the pause in Middle East hostilities eased geopolitical tensions.

item.title: CK Hutchison Starts London Arbitration Against Maersk Over Panama
item.description: CK Hutchison Holdings Ltd. has commenced arbitration against A.P. Moller-Maersk A/S after Panama’s forced takeover of its two ports in the country, intensifying a legal battle over control of the assets.

item.title: Shipowners Eye Hormuz Ceasefire Window for 800 Trapped Vessels
item.description: Shipowners are scrambling to understand the fine print in a ceasefire that could temporarily open up the Strait of Hormuz, hoping to take advantage of a potential window to extract more than 800 vessels trapped in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Korean Stocks, Won Surge After Iran Ceasefire Brings Relief
item.description: South Korean assets surged after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran, easing fears of prolonged disruptions to global energy supplies and triggering a broad risk-on move.

item.title: Copper Advances as Two-Week Hormuz Truce Eases Supply Worries
item.description: Copper rose after Iran agreed to temporarily reopen the Strait of Hormuz as part of a two-week ceasefire agreement with the US and Israel.

item.title: US Soybean Oil Futures Sink 5% on Iran Ceasefire Plan
item.description: Soybean oil futures sank 5% as a temporary ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran sparked a plunge in crude prices, denting the appeal of crop-based biofuels.

item.title: FTSE Keeps Indonesia’s Market Status Amid Ongoing Reforms
item.description: FTSE Russell will “closely monitor” Indonesia’s capital market reforms after the postponement of a March index review of the nation’s equities, as regulators seek to avert a possible downgrade by MSCI Inc.

item.title: US-Iran Ceasefire to Fuel Asia Relief Rally, Strategists Say
item.description: Donald Trump’s announcement of a two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran brings welcome relief to Asian markets, according to strategists. Equities should see strong rallies across the region in the near term, they say.

item.title: North Korea Fires Projectile After South Korean Leader’s Apology
item.description: North Korea fired an unidentified projectile on Wednesday, South Korea’s military said, just days after South Korean President Lee Jae Myung expressed regret over an incident involving drones that crossed the border into the North’s airspace.

item.title: Treasuries Rise as Ceasefire Spurs Oil Drop, Fed Rate-Cut Hopes
item.description: Short-dated notes led a jump in Treasuries after the US-Iran ceasefire pummeled oil prices and fueled hopes for the Federal Reserve to resume interest rate cuts.
